title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud presented a session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for standardized agent development. The session covered building agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB through unified connectors, plus implementing AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech session covers building agents with the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and integrating the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to standardize how agents connect to data and tools. It demonstrates agents that reach BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB through unified connectors, and it addresses AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring. The takeaways: MCP provides a common interface for tool access across these services, and ADK supports agent orchestration with operational tracking for cost and behavior.
